title: My Creative Journey
subtitle: Beyond the Grid: How a Campus Problem Launched My Design Career
excerpt: In September 2023, I entered campus as a first-year student and immediately noticed that student club posters were chaotic and failing to connect. Driven by a desire to fix this visual gap—and a practical need for pocket money—I began designing campus announcements. The true turning point came when I mentored under Peterson Gachii in the Christian Union, who introduced me to professional standards. By reverse-engineering workflows and conducting late-night research, I treated every club poster as a high-stakes design brief to master typography, balance, and color theory.What began as a student hustle quickly transformed into a deep obsession with visual strategy. Now in my third year, I have evolved those early campus experiments into a professional graphic design practice. I have shifted from simply making layouts look pretty to building cohesive, intentional visual systems that demand attention and deliver measurable results for clients.

title: Design Thinking
subtitle: Why Boring Minimalism is Officially Dead
excerpt: The Shift:For the past ten years, corporate graphic design felt incredibly safe and predictable. Every brand used the exact same clean geometric fonts, massive white spaces, and flat, muted colors. It was the peak of boredom minimalism. But as we look at the current creative landscape, the design world is finally fighting back.The Trend:We are currently witnessing a massive rise in Expressive Maximalism and Retro-Futurism. Brands are completely abandoning safe layouts in favor of:Heavy, distorted typography that pushes the boundaries of legibility.Gritty, analog textures that make digital screens feel tactile and human.Clashing, high-contrast color combinations inspired by early 90s cyber aesthetics.The Takeaway:In a digital world completely flooded with content, looking clean is no longer enough to get noticed. The future of graphic design belongs to brave visuals that value raw personality over perfect, safe neatness.
